title: Replace ticket-derived tx_id generation with issued transaction IDs
plan_sections: Server-owned transaction variables vs client-managed inputs
goal: Replace any ticket-derived or helper-derived transaction identity generation in runtime code with server-issued monotonic transaction IDs allocated only through the canonical begin path.
why_this_ticket: The 0.6.0 protocol requires tx_id to be exact, server-issued, opaque once created, and never derived from ticket_id or other client-managed labels. This ticket implements that requirement in runtime code and removes identity ambiguity from new writes.
scope: Identify all runtime code paths that create a new transaction identity.
Replace any ticket-derived, task-derived, session-derived, string-derived, or heuristic tx_id generation logic with issued monotonic IDs.
Ensure tx_id issuance occurs only through the canonical begin path for new transactions.
Ensure issued tx_id values are represented as integers in JSON-facing artifacts.
Align issuance behavior with tx_id counter metadata handling.
Preserve bounded compatibility for historical data without weakening strict new-write semantics.
out_of_scope: Redesigning the full materialized continuation contract.
Rewriting the full resume dispatcher around next_action.
Removing sentinel no-active handling except where directly required to stop tx_id misuse.
Broad regression-suite expansion beyond what is necessary to validate issued tx_id behavior.
expected_outputs: A runtime implementation where all new tx_id allocation uses a single canonical issuance path.
Removal or neutralization of any code path that derives tx_id from ticket_id or other client-managed labels.
Alignment between runtime issuance behavior and tx_id counter persistence.
A concise compatibility note describing how historical non-0.6.0 tx_id formats remain readable without affecting new writes.
implementation_requirements: tx_id must be integer-valued for new writes.
tx_id must not be derived from ticket_id, task_id, session_id, title, path, or any other client-managed label.
A new tx_id may be created only when starting a new transaction.
Resume must reuse the exact existing tx_id.
The issuance counter must remain monotonic.
Missing tx_id counter metadata may be treated as a zero baseline only where allowed by the plan.
Malformed tx_id counter metadata must fail clearly and must not be silently treated as zero.
acceptance_criteria: No new runtime code path derives tx_id from ticket_id.
No new runtime code path derives tx_id from task_id, session_id, title, or string heuristics.
Newly created transactions receive tx_id values only through canonical begin.
Resumed transactions reuse the exact existing tx_id and never mint a replacement.
Issued tx_id values are persisted in JSON-facing artifacts as integers.
Issuance behavior is backed by monotonic counter state and does not rely on ad hoc identity creation.
Any retained historical compatibility behavior remains read-path-only and does not redefine strict new-write semantics.
review_checkpoints: Confirm every transaction-creation path routes through a single issuance mechanism.
Confirm every resume path reuses an existing tx_id rather than allocating a new one.
Confirm no helper API label can influence canonical tx_id creation.
Confirm counter persistence and runtime issuance stay in sync under repeated begin operations.
Confirm historical compatibility logic does not leak into new-write behavior.
notes: This ticket is foundational for the rest of phase 2 because exact active-transaction continuation depends on exact issued identity.
If legacy tx_id formats remain readable historically, that compatibility must not allow new writes to emit legacy-shaped identities.
Prefer deleting identity-derivation heuristics over layering additional normalization rules on top of them.
